Documentation.html.title: Engine — IoT Swarm Mesh Simulation Engine | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
Documentation.html.summary:
Engine — IoT Swarm Mesh Simulation Engine The Engine library is the self-contained simulation core of the IoT Swarm Mesh project. It models a wireless IoT network on a 2D plane: devices are placed at arbitrary positions, data packets flow between them tick-by-tick, and a flooding broadcast algorithm propagates data toward the central hub. The library has no UI dependencies and can be driven by any host (console, Blazor server, test harness) that calls SimulationEngine.Instance.Tick(). Architecture overview graph TD SE["SimulationEngine
(singleton)
tick loop · device registry
packet priority queue · typed events"] PR["PacketRouter
(singleton)
flooding broadcast"] SS["SimulationStatistics
(singleton)
event-driven metric collector
history ring-buffer"] subgraph Devices D["Device (abstract)
identity · position
receive / forward logic"] Hub["HubDevice
network gateway"] Gen["GeneratorDevice
periodic data source"] Emit["EmitterDevice
command actuator"] end subgraph Packets P["Packet
routing metadata + TTL"] PD["PacketData
untyped payload"] CP["ConfirmationPacket
delivery ACK"] end subgraph Events["Core/SimulationEvents.cs"] EV["PacketRegistered
PacketDelivered
PacketExpired
DeviceRegistered
DeviceRemoved
Ticked"] end SE -- raises --> EV EV -- consumed by --> SS SE -->|TickEvent| Gen SE -->|GetVisibleDevicesFor| PR PR -->|RegisterPacket| SE D -->|Recieve → Route| PR Hub & Gen & Emit -->|extend| D Gen -->|creates| P CP -->|extends| P P -->|carries| PD Key concepts Tick-based time model SimulationEngine is the singleton clock. Each call to Tick() represents one simulation step: TickCount is incremented. Wall-clock delta time (dt) is measured. TickEvent is raised — subscribed devices perform periodic work (e.g. GeneratorDevice emitting a packet). All packets whose ArrivalTick ≤ TickCount are dequeued and dispatched to their NextHop device. Ticked is raised with the final tick count and dt for observers such as SimulationStatistics. Visibility & routing Two devices can communicate only when their Euclidean distance is within SimulationEngine.VisibilityDistance (default 200 units, configurable at runtime). PacketRouter implements a flooding broadcast: every packet is cloned and forwarded to all visible neighbours of the forwarding device. Packet lifetime A Packet carries a source (From), destination (To), next-hop device, arrival tick, and a TTL counter. Each hop decrements TTL; a packet reaching zero is silently dropped (raises PacketExpired). If NeedConfirmation is set, the destination automatically sends a ConfirmationPacket back to the originator. GeneratorDevice send cadence GeneratorDevice maintains an internal counter (CurrentTickCount) incremented on every TickEvent. When it reaches GenFrequencyTicks it resets to zero and one packet is emitted — so exactly one packet is sent every GenFrequencyTicks ticks. tick 1 … tick N-1 → counter increments, no packet tick N → counter resets to 0, packet sent to Hub tick N+1 … → cycle repeats Event-driven statistics SimulationStatistics subscribes to the typed events raised by SimulationEngine and maintains eight cumulative metrics. On every tick it appends a TickSnapshot to a ring-buffer (capped at 300 entries) for time-series charting. Observers subscribe to SimulationStatistics.Updated to react to metric changes without polling. Project structure Path Responsibility Core/SimulationEngine.cs Singleton engine: tick loop, device registry, packet priority queue, all typed events Core/SimulationEvents.cs Strongly-typed EventArgs classes for all engine events Devices/Device.cs Abstract base: identity, 2D position, receive / forward logic Devices/HubDevice.cs Central gateway; destination for all GeneratorDevice packets Devices/GeneratorDevice.cs Emits one packet per GenFrequencyTicks ticks Devices/EmitterDevice.cs Receives command packets and applies a boolean state Packets/Packet.cs Core transmission unit: routing metadata, TTL, payload Packets/PacketData.cs Untyped application-level payload wrapper Packets/ConfirmationPacket.cs Delivery acknowledgement routed back to the originator Routers/PacketRouter.cs Flooding broadcast router Statistics/SimulationStatistics.cs Event-driven singleton: eight metrics + history ring-buffer Statistics/StatMetric.cs Single observable metric with display formatting and plottable flag Statistics/TickSnapshot.cs Immutable per-tick value record for time-series charting Packet flow sequenceDiagram participant G as GeneratorDevice participant PR as PacketRouter participant SE as SimulationEngine participant N as Neighbour Device participant H as HubDevice participant SS as SimulationStatistics Note over G: every GenFrequencyTicks ticks G->>PR: Route(packet, self) PR->>SE: GetVisibleDevicesFor(G) SE-->>PR: [N, ...] PR->>SE: RegisterPacket(clone → NextHop=N) SE-->>SS: PacketRegistered Note over SE: tick advances SE->>N: NextHop.Recieve(packet) alt packet.To ≠ N (intermediate hop) N->>PR: Route(packet, N) PR->>SE: RegisterPacket(clone → NextHop=next) else packet.To = H (final delivery) SE-->>SS: PacketDelivered N->>H: Accept(packet) else TTL = 0 (dropped) SE-->>SS: PacketExpired end SE-->>SS: Ticked SS->>SS: AppendSnapshot → History Statistics extensibility To add a new tracked metric: Add a public StatMetric MyMetric { get; } property in SimulationStatistics and include it in the Metrics array. Subscribe to the relevant SimulationEngine event in the constructor and call MyMetric.Increment() or MyMetric.Set(value). If the metric should appear as a selectable chart series, pass isPlottable: true to the StatMetric constructor and add the corresponding TickSnapshot field with a mapping case in GetSnapshotValue.

api/Engine.Core.SimulationEngine.html.title: Class SimulationEngine | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Core.SimulationEngine.html.summary: Class SimulationEngine Namespace Engine.Core Assembly Engine.dll The central singleton that drives the entire simulation. It maintains the device registry, owns the tick-based time model, and manages the priority queue of in-flight packets. Every call to Tick() advances the simulation by one step: the tick counter is incremented, wall-clock delta time is measured, all subscribers to TickEvent are notified, and any packets whose ArrivalTick has been reached are dequeued and dispatched to their NextHop device. public class SimulationEngine Inheritance object SimulationEngine Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields VISIBILITY_DISTANCE The default visibility distance used when none is configured explicitly. public const int VISIBILITY_DISTANCE = 200 Field Value int Properties ActivePackets Gets a read-only snapshot of all packets currently in-flight. public IReadOnlyList<Packet> ActivePackets { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<Packet> Devices Gets a read-only snapshot of all devices registered in the simulation. public IReadOnlyList<Device> Devices { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<Device> Hub Gets the hub device registered in this simulation, or null if no HubDevice has been added yet. public Device? Hub { get; } Property Value Device Instance Gets the singleton instance of SimulationEngine. public static SimulationEngine Instance { get; } Property Value SimulationEngine TickCount Gets the total number of ticks that have elapsed since the simulation started. public long TickCount { get; } Property Value long VisibilityDistance The effective visibility distance for this simulation instance. Defaults to VISIBILITY_DISTANCE; can be changed at runtime. public int VisibilityDistance { get; set; } Property Value int Methods GetVisibleDevicesFor(Device) Returns all devices that are within VISIBILITY_DISTANCE of d, excluding d itself. Used by PacketRouter to determine broadcast targets. public IEnumerable<Device> GetVisibleDevicesFor(Device d) Parameters d Device The device whose neighbours are requested. Returns IEnumerable<Device> An enumerable of visible neighbour devices. RegisterDevice(Device) Adds a device to the simulation. If the device is a HubDevice it is also stored in Hub. public void RegisterDevice(Device device) Parameters device Device The device to register. RegisterPacket(Packet) Enqueues a packet into the in-flight priority queue. The absolute arrival tick is computed as TickCount + packet.TicksToTravel and stored on the packet. The queue is a min-heap keyed by arrival tick, so the earliest-arriving packet is always at the front. public void RegisterPacket(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The packet to schedule for delivery. RemoveDevice(Guid) Removes a previously registered device by its Id. If the removed device was the hub, Hub is set to null. public void RemoveDevice(Guid id) Parameters id Guid The unique identifier of the device to remove. Reset() Removes all registered devices and discards all in-flight packets, resetting the engine to its initial empty state. public void Reset() Tick() Advances the simulation by one tick. Increments TickCount. Measures wall-clock delta time since the last tick. Fires TickEvent so that subscribed devices can act. Dispatches all packets that are due to arrive this tick. public (long tick, double dt) Tick() Returns (long tick, double dt) A tuple of the current TickCount and the wall-clock milliseconds elapsed since the previous tick. Events DeviceRegistered Raised after a new device is added to the simulation. public event EventHandler<DeviceRegisteredEventArgs>? DeviceRegistered Event Type EventHandler<DeviceRegisteredEventArgs> DeviceRemoved Raised after a device is removed from the simulation. public event EventHandler<DeviceRemovedEventArgs>? DeviceRemoved Event Type EventHandler<DeviceRemovedEventArgs> PacketDelivered Raised when a packet reaches its intended destination device. public event EventHandler<PacketDeliveredEventArgs>? PacketDelivered Event Type EventHandler<PacketDeliveredEventArgs> PacketExpired Raised when a packet's TTL reaches zero and it is silently dropped. public event EventHandler<PacketExpiredEventArgs>? PacketExpired Event Type EventHandler<PacketExpiredEventArgs> PacketRegistered Raised every time a packet is enqueued (including flood clones). public event EventHandler<PacketRegisteredEventArgs>? PacketRegistered Event Type EventHandler<PacketRegisteredEventArgs> TickEvent Raised once per call to Tick(), after the tick counter has been incremented but before in-flight packets are dispatched. Devices such as GeneratorDevice subscribe to this event to perform periodic actions. public event EventHandler? TickEvent Event Type EventHandler Ticked Raised once per Tick() call after all packets are dispatched. public event EventHandler<TickedEventArgs>? Ticked Event Type EventHandler<TickedEventArgs>

api/Engine.Devices.Device.html.title: Class Device | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.Device.html.summary: Class Device Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll Abstract base class for all devices in the simulation network. A device has a unique identity and a position in 2D space. When it receives a packet it either forwards it (if it is not the intended recipient) or accepts and processes it. public abstract class Device Inheritance object Device Derived EmitterDevice GeneratorDevice HubDevice Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Id Gets the unique identifier of this device. public Guid Id { get; } Property Value Guid Name Gets or sets the human-readable name of this device. public string Name { get; set; } Property Value string Position Gets or sets the 2D position of this device in the simulation world. public Vector2 Position { get; set; } Property Value Vector2 Methods Accept(Packet) Processes a packet that is addressed to and has been delivered to this device. Derived classes implement the specific handling logic here. public abstract void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The delivered packet to process. Recieve(Packet) Called when a packet arrives at this device. If the packet is addressed to another device the router broadcasts it to all visible neighbours. If the packet is addressed to this device and delivery confirmation was requested, a ConfirmationPacket is sent back to the originator before Accept(Packet) is invoked. public void Recieve(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The packet that has arrived at this device.

api/Engine.Devices.EmitterDevice.html.title: Class EmitterDevice | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.EmitterDevice.html.summary: Class EmitterDevice Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll A device that receives command packets from the hub and applies a boolean state to itself (e.g. turning an actuator on or off). When an accepted packet carries a bool payload the device updates its State accordingly. public class EmitterDevice : Device Inheritance object Device EmitterDevice Inherited Members Device.Name Device.Id Device.Position Device.Recieve(Packet) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties State Gets the current on/off state of this emitter device. public bool State { get; protected set; } Property Value bool Methods Accept(Packet) Accepts a packet and, if the payload is a bool value, updates State to that value. public override void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The packet delivered to this device.

api/Engine.Devices.GeneratorDevice.html.title: Class GeneratorDevice | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.GeneratorDevice.html.summary: Class GeneratorDevice Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll A device that periodically generates sensor-like data packets and routes them toward the hub. An internal tick counter increments on every engine tick and resets to zero after it reaches GenFrequencyTicks; a packet is emitted only at that reset moment, so exactly one packet is sent per GenFrequencyTicks ticks. public class GeneratorDevice : Device Inheritance object Device GeneratorDevice Inherited Members Device.Name Device.Id Device.Position Device.Recieve(Packet) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors GeneratorDevice(long) Initialises the generator and subscribes to the engine tick event. public GeneratorDevice(long genFrequency) Parameters genFrequency long The interval in engine ticks at which new packets are generated. Properties CurrentTickCount Gets the number of ticks that have elapsed since the last packet was sent. Resets to zero after it reaches GenFrequencyTicks. public long CurrentTickCount { get; } Property Value long GenFrequencyTicks Gets or sets the number of engine ticks between consecutive packet generation events. public long GenFrequencyTicks { get; set; } Property Value long Methods Accept(Packet) Generator devices do not consume incoming packets; this method is a no-op. public override void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The packet addressed to this device (ignored).

api/Engine.Devices.HubDevice.html.title: Class HubDevice | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.HubDevice.html.summary: Class HubDevice Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll Represents the central hub (gateway) device of the simulation network. The hub is the designated destination for packets generated by GeneratorDevice instances and the root of the network topology. It is registered as the singleton hub inside SimulationEngine when added to the simulation. public class HubDevice : Device Inheritance object Device HubDevice Inherited Members Device.Name Device.Id Device.Position Device.Recieve(Packet) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Accept(Packet) Accepts a packet that has been delivered to the hub and logs it. public override void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The delivered packet.

api/Engine.Devices.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Devices Classes Device Abstract base class for all devices in the simulation network. A device has a unique identity and a position in 2D space. When it receives a packet it either forwards it (if it is not the intended recipient) or accepts and processes it. EmitterDevice A device that receives command packets from the hub and applies a boolean state to itself (e.g. turning an actuator on or off). When an accepted packet carries a bool payload the device updates its State accordingly. GeneratorDevice A device that periodically generates sensor-like data packets and routes them toward the hub. An internal tick counter increments on every engine tick and resets to zero after it reaches GenFrequencyTicks; a packet is emitted only at that reset moment, so exactly one packet is sent per GenFrequencyTicks ticks. HubDevice Represents the central hub (gateway) device of the simulation network. The hub is the designated destination for packets generated by GeneratorDevice instances and the root of the network topology. It is registered as the singleton hub inside SimulationEngine when added to the simulation.

api/Engine.Packets.ConfirmationPacket.ht.title: Class ConfirmationPacket | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.ConfirmationPacket.ht.summary: Class ConfirmationPacket Namespace Engine.Packets Assembly Engine.dll A special packet that is automatically generated and sent back to the original sender when a packet with NeedConfirmation set to true is successfully delivered. The payload contains the Guid bytes of the confirmed packet, allowing the sender to correlate the acknowledgement with the original transmission. public class ConfirmationPacket : Packet Inheritance object Packet ConfirmationPacket Inherited Members Packet.Id Packet.TicksToTravel Packet.ArrivalTick Packet.NextHop Packet.TTL Packet.From Packet.To Packet.Payload Packet.NeedConfirmation Packet.Clone() Packet.Equals(object) Packet.GetHashCode() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors ConfirmationPacket(Packet) Initialises a new confirmation packet as a reply to original. The source and destination are swapped — the confirmation travels from the original destination back to the original sender. public ConfirmationPacket(Packet original) Parameters original Packet The packet whose delivery is being acknowledged.

api/Engine.Packets.Packet.html.title: Class Packet | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.Packet.html.summary: Class Packet Namespace Engine.Packets Assembly Engine.dll Represents a unit of data travelling through the simulation network. A packet knows its originator (From), its intended destination (To), and the NextHop device it will be delivered to on the next simulation tick. Travel time is expressed in engine ticks via TicksToTravel, and each hop decrements the TTL counter — a packet that reaches zero TTL is silently dropped. public class Packet Inheritance object Packet Derived ConfirmationPacket Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors Packet(Device, Device, PacketData) Represents a unit of data travelling through the simulation network. A packet knows its originator (From), its intended destination (To), and the NextHop device it will be delivered to on the next simulation tick. Travel time is expressed in engine ticks via TicksToTravel, and each hop decrements the TTL counter — a packet that reaches zero TTL is silently dropped. public Packet(Device from, Device to, PacketData payload) Parameters from Device to Device payload PacketData Properties ArrivalTick Gets or sets the absolute engine tick at which this packet is due to arrive at NextHop. Set by RegisterPacket(Packet) when the packet is enqueued. public long ArrivalTick { get; set; } Property Value long From Gets the device that originally sent this packet. public Device From { get; } Property Value Device Id Gets the unique identifier of this packet instance. public Guid Id { get; } Property Value Guid NeedConfirmation Gets or sets a value indicating whether the destination device should send a ConfirmationPacket back to From upon successful delivery. public bool NeedConfirmation { get; set; } Property Value bool NextHop Gets or sets the device that will receive this packet on arrival. public Device NextHop { get; set; } Property Value Device Payload Gets the data payload carried by this packet. public PacketData Payload { get; } Property Value PacketData TTL Gets or sets the time-to-live counter. Decremented on every hop; the packet is dropped when it reaches zero. public int TTL { get; set; } Property Value int TicksToTravel Gets or sets the number of engine ticks the packet takes to travel between two adjacent devices. public long TicksToTravel { get; set; } Property Value long To Gets the intended destination device for this packet. public Device To { get; } Property Value Device Methods Clone() Creates a shallow copy of this packet, suitable for broadcasting the same packet to multiple next-hop neighbours. public Packet Clone() Returns Packet A shallow clone of this packet. Equals(object?) Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current object. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object The object to compare with the current object. Returns bool true if the specified object is equal to the current object; otherwise, false. GetHashCode() Serves as the default hash function. public override int GetHashCode() Returns int A hash code for the current object.

api/Engine.Packets.PacketData.html.title: Class PacketData | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.PacketData.html.summary: Class PacketData Namespace Engine.Packets Assembly Engine.dll Carries the application-level payload of a Packet. The Data property is intentionally untyped so that any value (sensor readings, command flags, raw bytes, etc.) can be transported without the engine imposing a schema. public class PacketData Inheritance object PacketData Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Data Gets or sets the payload value. The consuming device is responsible for casting this to the expected type. public object? Data { get; set; } Property Value object

api/Engine.Routers.PacketRouter.html.title: Class PacketRouter | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.PacketRouter.html.summary: Class PacketRouter Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Implements a flooding broadcast routing strategy. When asked to route a packet from a given device, the router queries SimulationEngine for all devices visible to that device and enqueues a cloned copy of the packet addressed to each of them. This is the sole routing mechanism currently used by the engine. public class PacketRouter Inheritance object PacketRouter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Instance Gets the singleton instance of PacketRouter. public static PacketRouter Instance { get; } Property Value PacketRouter Methods Route(Packet, Device) Broadcasts packet to every device that is within VisibilityDistance of device. Each neighbour receives its own shallow clone so that per-hop fields (such as NextHop and ArrivalTick) can be set independently. public void Route(Packet packet, Device device) Parameters packet Packet The packet to broadcast. device Device The device that is currently holding the packet.

api/Engine.Routers.html.title: Namespace Engine.Routers | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Routers Classes PacketRouter Implements a flooding broadcast routing strategy. When asked to route a packet from a given device, the router queries SimulationEngine for all devices visible to that device and enqueues a cloned copy of the packet addressed to each of them. This is the sole routing mechanism currently used by the engine.

api/Engine.Statistics.SimulationStatisti.title: Class SimulationStatistics | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Statistics.SimulationStatisti.summary: Class SimulationStatistics Namespace Engine.Statistics Assembly Engine.dll A singleton that listens to SimulationEngine events and accumulates simulation metrics. Extensibility: add a new StatMetric to Metrics and subscribe to any SimulationEngine event to populate it. The Updated event is raised after every change so UI layers can re-render reactively without polling. Every tick a TickSnapshot is appended to History (capped at MaxHistoryLength entries) for time-series charts. public sealed class SimulationStatistics Inheritance object SimulationStatistics Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields MaxHistoryLength Maximum number of per-tick snapshots retained in History. public const int MaxHistoryLength = 300 Field Value int Properties ActivePackets Number of packets currently in-flight in the simulation priority queue. public StatMetric ActivePackets { get; } Property Value StatMetric AvgTickMs Running arithmetic mean of wall-clock milliseconds spent per Tick() call. public StatMetric AvgTickMs { get; } Property Value StatMetric DeliveryRate Ratio of delivered packets to the total of delivered and expired packets, expressed as a percentage. Computed as TotalPacketsDelivered / (TotalPacketsDelivered + TotalPacketsExpired) ? 100. public StatMetric DeliveryRate { get; } Property Value StatMetric History Per-tick snapshots in chronological order, capped at MaxHistoryLength. public IReadOnlyList<TickSnapshot> History { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<TickSnapshot> Instance Gets the singleton instance. public static SimulationStatistics Instance { get; } Property Value SimulationStatistics Metrics All metrics in declaration order � drives the summary card grid. public IReadOnlyList<StatMetric> Metrics { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<StatMetric> PlottableMetrics Metrics that can be selected as a chart series. public IReadOnlyList<StatMetric> PlottableMetrics { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<StatMetric> TotalDevicesAdded Cumulative number of devices registered in the engine since the last reset. public StatMetric TotalDevicesAdded { get; } Property Value StatMetric TotalPacketsDelivered Packets that successfully reached their intended destination device. public StatMetric TotalPacketsDelivered { get; } Property Value StatMetric TotalPacketsExpired Packets discarded because their TTL counter reached zero before delivery. public StatMetric TotalPacketsExpired { get; } Property Value StatMetric TotalPacketsRegistered Total packets enqueued into the simulation, including every flood clone. public StatMetric TotalPacketsRegistered { get; } Property Value StatMetric TotalTicks Total engine ticks elapsed since the last reset. public StatMetric TotalTicks { get; } Property Value StatMetric Methods GetSnapshotValue(TickSnapshot, StatMetric, IReadOnlyList<StatMetric>) Extracts the value for the given metric from a TickSnapshot. Used by the chart to be series-agnostic. public static double GetSnapshotValue(TickSnapshot snapshot, StatMetric metric, IReadOnlyList<StatMetric> plottable) Parameters snapshot TickSnapshot metric StatMetric plottable IReadOnlyList<StatMetric> Returns double Reset() Resets all metric counters and clears the history. public void Reset() Events Updated Raised after any metric value changes. public event Action? api/Engine.Statistics.html.title: Namespace Engine.Statistics | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Statistics.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Statistics Classes SimulationStatistics A singleton that listens to SimulationEngine events and accumulates simulation metrics. Extensibility: add a new StatMetric to Metrics and subscribe to any SimulationEngine event to populate it. The Updated event is raised after every change so UI layers can re-render reactively without polling. Every tick a TickSnapshot is appended to History (capped at MaxHistoryLength entries) for time-series charts. StatMetric A single observable metric tracked by SimulationStatistics. UI layers bind to DisplayValue and listen to Updated for re-renders. TickSnapshot An immutable snapshot of all plottable metric values captured at the end of a single engine tick. Stored in the SimulationStatistics history ring-buffer and consumed by the chart UI.

index.html.title: IoT Swarm Mesh � Engine Documentation | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
index.html.summary: IoT Swarm Mesh � Engine Documentation Welcome to the IoT Swarm Mesh simulation engine documentation. This site is generated automatically from source XML comments and the hand-written conceptual guide using DocFX. Quick links Conceptual overview & architecture API Reference About the project The Engine library is the self-contained simulation core of the IoT Swarm Mesh project. It models a wireless IoT network on a 2D plane: devices are placed at arbitrary positions, data packets flow between them tick-by-tick, and a flooding broadcast algorithm propagates data toward the central hub. The library has no UI dependencies and can be driven by any host (console, Blazor WebAssembly, test harness) that calls SimulationEngine.Instance.Tick(). Source code: github.com/kamen4/IoT-Swarm-Mesh
